overview: This image features a male Mountain Bluebird (Sialia currucoides). Male Mountain Bluebirds are recognized by their bright turquoise-blue plumage and lighter-colored underbellies. They are migratory thrushes commonly found in the mountainous regions of western North America. These birds typically forage for insects from perches like tree branches, fence posts, or power lines. iNaturalist +3 4 sites Mountain Bluebird (Sialia currucoides) · iNaturalist
